text:
Market today: The market mood is cautious. Headlines suggest earnings aren't enough to drive gains and surging IPO activity hints at a potential bubble. This signals a tougher environment for your concentrated tech/chip book.

Your holdings: SOXL is down 36.74% and SPAL (a brand-new, very volatile IPO) is down 55.27%. Both are leveraged and decaying, and on margin, these drawdowns are amplified and dangerous. QQQ and QQQI are also flagged for trimming.

Watch: Keep a very close eye on your leveraged positions, SOXL and SPAL. Also, watch for any further signs of softening tech sentiment.

Bottom line: Focus on risk management, especially with your leveraged ETFs, as market enthusiasm for tech seems to be waning.
plays: TRIM QQQ at its stop of 670, and QQQI at 52, to reduce exposure as flagged by your scanner.
Review SOXL and SPAL. These leveraged ETFs are decaying and showing massive losses; consider if their risk profiles still fit your margin account.
No new buys today - hold and watch. The market sentiment is cautious, and there's no strong external catalyst to act now.
